item.title: Short, not skinny: The distinct sarcopenia phenotype of pediatric primary sclerosing cholangitis-inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Sarcopenia is prevalent in pediatric liver disease and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and predicts adverse outcomes. We aimed to compare sarcopenia prevalence in pediatric primary sclerosing chol...

item.title: Primary sclerosing cholangitis displays distinct colonic mucosa topography yet a shared mast cell state with ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Nature communications - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic, progressing cholestatic disease that often co-occurs with inflammatory bowel disease (PSC-IBD). PSC-IBD affecting the colon (PSC-ulcerative coliti...

item.title: Recent Advances in the Pharmacological Management of Pediatric Spondyloarthritis.
item.description: Paediatric drugs - Review - Juvenile spondyloarthritis (JSpA) refers to a heterogeneous group of inflammatory diseases affecting peripheral joints and the axial skeleton during childhood and adolescence. Enthesitis-related ar...
